The latest quarter’s higher sales, earnings and a small dividend lift reinforce that story, but do not materially change the near term focus on converting backlog efficiently. data.title: ATI Aerospace And Defense Growth Tests High-Margin Earnings Story
data.description:
• ATI (NYSE:ATI) reported strong fourth quarter results, supported by demand for aerospace and defense products.
  • The company highlighted expanded long term agreements tied to next generation jet engines and missile programs.
  • Management pointed to proprietary alloys as a key factor in higher margins and a shift in product mix.

ATI enters this update with its shares at $143.93 and a very large 5 year return, reflecting substantial compounding over that period. Over the past year the stock is up 135.8%, including gains of 7.8% over the past week and 15.7% over the past month, while year to date performance sits at 20.7%. These moves frame the latest quarter as one more data point investors can weigh against an already strong share price run.

The company is putting clear emphasis on defense related growth, longer term customer commitments, and higher margin proprietary products. For you as an investor, the key questions from here are how durable that demand in aerospace and defense proves to be, and how much further ATI can shift its mix toward these higher margin offerings in coming quarters.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for ATI by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on ATI.

2 things going right for ATI that this headline doesn't cover.

For you as an investor, the key takeaway from this update is the tension between ATI’s product mix story and its near term profit trends. Fourth quarter sales were roughly flat year on year at US$1,177.1 million versus US$1,172.7 million, yet net income in that period moved from US$137.1 million to US$96.6 million, with diluted EPS from continuing operations at US$0.69 compared to US$0.94. Over the full year, however, ATI reported higher sales of US$4,587.4 million versus US$4,362.1 million and higher net income of US$404.3 million compared to US$367.8 million, which suggests the broader direction for the year was positive even though the final quarter was softer on earnings.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The ATI Narrative

  • The focus on proprietary alloys, next generation jet engines and long term aerospace and defense agreements ties directly to the narrative that higher margin, contracted volumes can support more stable earnings across cycles.
  • The fourth quarter profit step down, despite resilient sales, is a reminder that execution on efficiency and capital intensive projects is not guaranteed to be smooth, which tests the thesis that margin strength alone will carry results.
  • The latest buyback tranche completion, and the pause in repurchases in late 2025, touches on capital allocation and balance sheet flexibility, an angle that is not fully explored in the existing narrative around contracts and capacity investments.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for ATI to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Reported fourth quarter net income and EPS were lower than the prior year period, which shows that higher margin products do not always translate into quarter on quarter earnings strength.
  • ⚠️ ATI relies heavily on aerospace and defense demand, and on a relatively concentrated set of customers, so shifts in ordering patterns or program timing at major peers like Howmet Aerospace or Precision Castparts could affect volumes.
  • 🎁 Full year sales and net income were higher than the previous year, supported by demand in next generation jet engines and missile programs, which reinforces the role of these end markets as important earnings drivers.
  • 🎁 Expanded long term agreements tied to proprietary alloys may give ATI better volume visibility and pricing power versus peers such as Carpenter Technology and Allegheny based competitors that lack the same contract depth.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to track how quickly ATI converts its contract wins and proprietary alloy positioning into consistent quarterly earnings, not just full year gains. That includes watching future commentary on capacity expansion, cost efficiency and any updates on aerospace and defense program ramps. It is also worth keeping an eye on capital allocation, including whether share repurchases resume and how that interacts with debt and investment needs.

data.title: Is Ascendis Pharma (ASND) Still Attractive After Its Strong Multi Year Share Price Run?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Ascendis Pharma's share price still reflects fair value after its recent run, this article will walk through what the numbers are saying right now.
  • The stock last closed at US$216.32, with returns of 5.1% over the past 30 days and 50.1% over the past year, while the 7-day return of a 2.6% decline shows the ride has not been perfectly smooth.
  • Recent moves sit against a backdrop of ongoing interest in Ascendis Pharma as a Nasdaq-listed pharmaceuticals and biotech company. Investors are watching how its product pipeline and clinical progress translate into long term potential. This broader context helps explain why the share price has seen both shorter term pullbacks and stronger multi year performance, including a 3-year return of 85.4% and a 5-year return of 36.4%.
  • On Simply Wall St's 6 point valuation check framework, Ascendis Pharma scores 4 out of 6 for being assessed as undervalued on four checks. This sets us up to compare different valuation methods next and then look at an even more complete way to think about what that score really means for you.

Ascendis Pharma delivered 50.1% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Biotechs industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Ascendis Pharma Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes the cash Ascendis Pharma is expected to generate in the future, then discounts those cash flows back to today to estimate what the business could be worth right now.

Using Simply Wall St's 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, Ascendis Pharma's latest twelve month free cash flow is about €47.3m. Analysts contribute detailed projections out to 2030, with free cash flow for that year estimated at €1,355.0m, and further years extrapolated by Simply Wall St rather than by analysts directly.

When those projected cash flows, including the ten year path from 2026 to 2035, are discounted back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of €833.1 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$216.32, this implies the stock is assessed as 74.0% undervalued on this DCF view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Ascendis Pharma is undervalued by 74.0%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Ascendis Pharma.

Approach 2: Ascendis Pharma Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings are not a steady guide, the P/S ratio is often more useful because it compares the share price with the revenue the business is already generating. It still reflects what the market is willing to pay for each unit of sales, and that tends to move with expectations for growth and perceived risk. Higher growth or lower risk can justify a higher “normal” P/S, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower one.

Ascendis Pharma currently trades on a P/S of 15.53x, compared with the Biotechs industry average of 11.28x and a peer group average of 24.95x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Ascendis is 13.36x. This is its proprietary estimate of what a balanced P/S might look like given the company’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk factors. This Fair Ratio aims to give you a cleaner reference point than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for those company specific features.

Set against that Fair Ratio, Ascendis Pharma’s current 15.53x P/S is higher, which points to the stock being priced above this modelled fair level on a sales based view.

Result: OVERVALUED

data.title: Bain Sale And Product Awards Reframe Coherent Ownership And Growth Story
data.description:
• Bain Capital plans to sell a significant portion of its stake in Coherent, reshaping the shareholder mix at NYSE:COHR.
  • Coherent received six awards in the 2026 Lightwave Innovation Reviews for its optical networking and photonics products.
  • These developments coincide with a current share price of $217.23 and notable share price moves over the past year.

For investors following Coherent, the twin updates on ownership and product recognition arrive after a very large 1-year return and a 382.8% return over 3 years. The stock is trading at $217.23, with a 30-day return of 13.7% and an 11.8% return year to date, while the past week shows a 4.6% decline. This combination of short-term pullback and strong longer-term gains frames how the Bain sale and industry awards might be viewed.

Looking ahead, you may want to watch how the Bain Capital divestment affects trading liquidity and the broader shareholder base, as well as any commentary from Coherent on future capital needs or partnerships. At the same time, the six Lightwave Innovation Reviews awards highlight product areas that could be important for Coherent's positioning in optical networking and photonics, and may factor into how investors assess the business over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Coherent by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Coherent.

2 things going right for Coherent that this headline doesn't cover.

Bain Capital’s plan to sell up to US$2.3b of Coherent stock sits next to a very different type of update, a repeat showing at the 2026 Lightwave Innovation Reviews. For you as an existing or potential shareholder, the Bain block sale mainly affects who owns the stock and how trading behaves, rather than the day to day operations. Large private equity holders often recycle capital after a period of strong share price performance, so the key questions are how much stock the market absorbs, at what discount, and whether Coherent uses any dip to repurchase shares or reshape its own capital structure. The awards, by contrast, point to product strength in optical networking and photonics where Coherent competes with names like Lumentum and Broadcom. That may help support relationships with AI data center and communications customers that have been driving recent revenue and earnings.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Coherent Narrative

  • The product awards align with the narrative focus on AI data center and high performance communications demand, reinforcing Coherent’s role in advanced optical transceivers and photonics components.
  • Bain’s planned stake reduction could challenge the narrative if it raises questions about long term commitment from a major shareholder, especially given the capital needs for manufacturing projects such as six inch indium phosphide.
  • The block sale and any shift in shareholder mix are not fully reflected in the narrative’s emphasis on execution risk around manufacturing and customer concentration, even though ownership changes can influence how those risks are financed over time.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Coherent to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ A large secondary sale by Bain Capital may create share price pressure around the transaction and raises questions about how quickly Coherent might need to finance future capacity expansion from other sources.
  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged 3 key risks for Coherent, including exposure to pricing pressure and capital intensive manufacturing, which could matter if demand or customer orders become less predictable.
  • 🎁 Coherent has been growing profit and revenue, with recent quarters supported by AI driven data center and communications demand, which can help support internal investments in new manufacturing capacity.
  • 🎁 The repeat showing of six Lightwave Innovation Reviews awards suggests Coherent’s product set in optical networking and photonics remains competitive against peers like Lumentum and Broadcom, which can help sustain customer relationships in high growth areas.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth tracking how the Bain block sale is priced, how quickly the market absorbs those shares, and whether Coherent chooses to participate via buybacks or related capital moves. On the business side, keep an eye on whether award winning transceivers and photonics products translate into continued orders and revenue within the company’s US$1.70b to US$1.84b revenue guidance range for the next quarter. You may also want to monitor commentary on AI data center and communications demand, progress on six inch indium phosphide capacity, and any signs that Coherent is gaining or losing share against competitors in high speed optics.

data.title: Otsuka Holdings (TSE:4578) EPS Slowdown Challenges Long Term Growth Narrative
data.description:
Otsuka Holdings (TSE:4578) has wrapped up FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of ¥650,149 million and basic EPS of ¥124.01, set against trailing twelve month figures of ¥2.47 trillion in revenue and EPS of ¥685.06. The company reported quarterly revenue of ¥599,785 million in Q4 2024 and ¥650,149 million in Q4 2025, while basic EPS moved from ¥281.41 to ¥124.01 over the same period, giving investors a view of how the top and bottom lines have shifted through the year. With a reported net profit margin at 14.7%, the latest earnings snapshot keeps the focus on how efficiently Otsuka is converting its global healthcare franchise into profit.

See our full analysis for Otsuka Holdings.

With the headline numbers reported, the next step is to see how these results line up with the most widely held narratives about Otsuka, highlighting where the story around growth, risk, and profitability aligns and where it appears different.

See what the community is saying about Otsuka Holdings

Advertisement

TTM earnings growth slows to 5.8%

  • Over the last 12 months, earnings growth is described at 5.8% compared with an average of 26.2% per year over the past five years, so the more recent pace is much lower than the longer term track record.
  • Consensus narrative talks about sustained revenue and profit support from CNS and specialty drugs like REXULTI and JYNARQUE. However, the step down from 26.2% to 5.8% earnings growth suggests the current profit trajectory is less aggressive than that longer term story, even though trailing twelve month EPS of ¥685.06 still sits above the FY 2025 quarterly EPS run rate.

Margins steady at 14.7% while growth forecasts cool

  • Net profit margin is reported at 14.7%, the same level as last year. Forward looking numbers based on trailing data point to only about 1.06% annual earnings growth and 1.4% annual revenue growth, both described as slower than the broader Japanese market.
  • Bulls argue that expanding late stage assets such as sibeprenlimab and new indications for REXULTI, together with acquisitions like Jnana and CAN10, can reshape Otsuka into higher value specialty areas. However, the modest 1.06% and 1.4% growth assumptions imply that, in the near term, these pipeline assets are not fully reflected in the baseline expectations and that the flat 14.7% margin profile is not yet showing the stronger margin expansion that the bullish view anticipates.
    • Consensus narrative also refers to global expansion and category growth in nutraceuticals supporting steady earnings, but the relatively low forecast growth rates suggest analysts are treating that contribution as gradual rather than rapid.
    • The unchanged margin level means there is currently no reported compression to back the more cautious idea of sharply shrinking profitability, but it also does not yet show the step up that optimistic scenarios describe.

With margins holding steady but forecast growth muted, bulls and consensus both lean on the pipeline and new categories to argue for stronger long term outcomes than the baseline numbers alone suggest, and you can see how that story is built out in the 🐂 Otsuka Holdings Bull Case

P/E of 15.4x with price just under DCF fair value

  • Otsuka is described as trading on a P/E of 15.4x, versus a peer average of 31.4x and an industry average of 15.4x, and the current share price of ¥10,585 sits slightly below a DCF fair value of ¥10,637.36.
  • Bears point to slower forward growth, with revenues expected to decline by 2.2% per year and margins shrinking to 8.3% in their scenario, and argue that dependence on a small set of drugs and higher R&D and acquisition spend could justify that cautious stance. However, the present 14.7% margin and P/E roughly in line with the broader pharmaceutical group indicate the market is not pricing in the sharper margin compression and revenue contraction that the bearish narrative sketches out.
    • The bearish case also references risks from cost controls and generics, but the combination of a P/E at the industry level and a price close to DCF fair value suggests current pricing is more aligned with moderate outcomes than with a severe deterioration.
    • At the same time, the relatively low forecast growth rates give bears some support, because they show that expectations are already framed around slower expansion rather than a continuation of the 26.2% five year earnings growth trend.

If you are weighing that cautious view against the current valuation, skeptics’ arguments about patent and pricing pressure are laid out in more depth in the 🐻 Otsuka Holdings Bear Case

data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Strategy's current share price still reflects its true worth, you are not alone. This article will walk through what the available numbers and tools are actually saying about value today.
  • Strategy closed at US$133.88, with returns of a 0.8% decline over 7 days, a 22.9% decline over 30 days, a 14.8% decline year to date, a 60.4% decline over 1 year, and a very large gain over 3 years that sits well above its 38.9% return over 5 years.
  • Recent coverage around Strategy has focused on its role as a software name with a history that has attracted both long term followers and short term traders. This helps explain why the share price has seen periods of sharp moves in both directions. That backdrop is important context if you are trying to work out whether current levels reflect cautious sentiment, optimism, or simply a reset after earlier enthusiasm.
  • On our framework of six valuation checks, Strategy scores 4 out of 6. You can see the breakdown in our valuation score. Next we will walk through the standard valuation approaches behind that result and then finish with a way to look at value that goes beyond any single model.

Find out why Strategy's -60.4% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Strategy Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of the cash a company could generate in the future and discounts those cash flows back to today using a required rate of return. The result is an estimate of what the business might be worth in total, and then on a per share basis.

For Strategy, the model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow figure is a loss of $80.0 million. Looking ahead, analysts and extrapolated estimates point to projected free cash flows that reach $6.2 billion in 2035, with a path that includes $9.3 billion in 2026 and $7.3 billion in 2028. Simply Wall St only receives direct analyst numbers for the earlier years, with the outer years extrapolated from those inputs.

Putting all those projected cash flows together and discounting them back to today produces an estimated intrinsic value of $274.90 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$133.88, this output suggests the stock is 51.3% undervalued on this DCF view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Strategy is undervalued by 51.3%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Strategy.

Approach 2: Strategy Price vs Book

For established, profitable companies, price based ratios are often a simple way to sanity check more complex models. Price to Book, or P/B, is especially useful when the balance sheet and asset base are a key part of the story, because it compares what the market is paying to the accounting value of net assets.

In general, higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/B ratio. Slower growth or higher risk usually line up with a lower, or discounted, P/B. So the question is not whether a P/B is high or low in isolation, but whether it makes sense given the company’s profile.

Strategy currently trades on a P/B of 1.01x. That sits below the Software industry average of 2.70x and also below the peer group average of 6.47x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what P/B might be reasonable for Strategy given factors such as its growth outlook, profit margins, size, risks and industry. Because the Fair Ratio blends all of these inputs for this specific company, it is designed to be more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or sector averages. On this measure, Strategy’s current P/B is assessed as below a Fair Ratio level.

Result: UNDERVALUED

data.description:

Even after such a large drop in price, it's still not a stretch to say that Cettire's price-to-sales (or "P/S") ratio of 0.2x right now seems quite "middle-of-the-road" compared to the Specialty Retail industry in Australia, where the median P/S ratio is around 0.6x. Although, it's not wise to simply ignore the P/S without explanation as investors may be disregarding a distinct opportunity or a costly mistake.

What Does Cettire's P/S Mean For Shareholders?

While the industry has experienced revenue growth lately, Cettire's revenue has gone into reverse gear, which is not great. It might be that many expect the dour revenue performance to strengthen positively, which has kept the P/S from falling. However, if this isn't the case, investors might get caught out paying too much for the stock.

Do Revenue Forecasts Match The P/S Ratio?

In order to justify its P/S ratio, Cettire would need to produce growth that's similar to the industry.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered virtually the same number to the company's top line as the year before. Still, the latest three year period has seen an excellent 254% overall rise in revenue, in spite of its uninspiring short-term performance. Accordingly, shareholders will be pleased, but also have some questions to ponder about the last 12 months.

Looking ahead now, revenue is anticipated to climb by 4.7% per year during the coming three years according to the dual analysts following the company. That's shaping up to be materially lower than the 11% each year growth forecast for the broader industry.

With this information, we find it interesting that Cettire is trading at a fairly similar P/S compared to the industry. It seems most investors are ignoring the fairly limited growth expectations and are willing to pay up for exposure to the stock. These shareholders may be setting themselves up for future disappointment if the P/S falls to levels more in line with the growth outlook.

Following Cettire's share price tumble, its P/S is just clinging on to the industry median P/S. We'd say the price-to-sales ratio's power isn't primarily as a valuation instrument but rather to gauge current investor sentiment and future expectations.

data.title: USA Rare Earth Shifts From Funding Story To Execution With U.S. Backing
data.description:
• USA Rare Earth (NasdaqGM:USAR) has entered into a landmark partnership with the U.S. government that includes significant funding support.
  • As part of the agreement, the government plans to take an equity stake in the company.
  • The funding is intended to accelerate development of a domestic rare earth mine and magnet manufacturing facility.

For investors watching USA Rare Earth at a current share price of $19.395, this government partnership represents a material shift in how the company is positioned within the rare earth supply chain. The stock has returned 37.1% year to date and 71.6% over the past year, with a mixed shorter term profile that includes a 9.6% gain over 30 days and an 11.2% decline over the past week. These moves frame a company that is already on many radars as this new funding and ownership structure emerges.

Looking ahead, the combination of federal capital and an equity stake gives USA Rare Earth a different risk profile than a typical early stage resource and processing project. For you as an investor or observer, the key questions now are how quickly the mine and magnet facility move toward production, how the ownership structure evolves, and how this partnership influences future financing options for NasdaqGM:USAR.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for USA Rare Earth by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on USA Rare Earth.

We've flagged 5 risks for USA Rare Earth. See which could impact your investment.

This government partnership effectively moves USA Rare Earth from a pure funding story toward an execution story. The package, which includes a planned 8% to 16% federal equity stake alongside a US$1.3b loan and US$277m in funding, addresses a large part of the roughly US$4.1b project cost for the Round Top mine in Texas and the magnet plant in Oklahoma. For you, that means less uncertainty around whether the company can finance a mine-to-magnet supply chain, and more focus on whether management can deliver on construction, commissioning and eventual operations.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ USA Rare Earth is still pre revenue, with less than US$1m in sales, so the investment case is tied to future execution rather than current cash generation.
  • ⚠️ Shareholders have been substantially diluted over the past year, and government ownership introduces political and policy risk if priorities change.
  • 🎁 Access to roughly US$3.1b to US$3.5b of capital, including government support, reduces financing uncertainty for building out the mine, processing and magnet facilities.
  • 🎁 A fully domestic rare earth mine and magnet chain could position USA Rare Earth competitively against players like MP Materials, Lynas Rare Earths and other magnet suppliers that serve EV, renewable energy and defense demand.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, the key things to watch are project milestones at Stillwater and Round Top, any changes to the terms or size of the government stake, and further capital raises or dilution as the company progresses. You may also want to track how USA Rare Earth’s plans compare with developments at peers, and whether offtake agreements or long term contracts with auto, wind, or defense customers are announced, because those can help support future cash flow visibility.

data.title: Is Allegiant Travel (ALGT) Pricing Reflect Recent Share Surge And Travel Sector Headlines
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Allegiant Travel's current share price really reflects its underlying worth, you are not alone. That is exactly what this article will unpack.
  • The stock last closed at US$111.30, with returns of 23.7% over 30 days, 26.5% year to date, 33.7% over 1 year, 14.7% over 3 years, and a 52.6% decline over 5 years. Taken together, these figures paint a mixed picture of recent performance and shifting risk perceptions.
  • Recent news around Allegiant Travel has focused on its position in the U.S. travel sector and how investor sentiment is responding to broader conditions in air travel and leisure demand. These headlines help explain why the share price has moved sharply over shorter periods even as longer term returns look very different.
  • On our valuation framework Allegiant Travel scores 3 out of 6 on undervaluation checks. We will look at what different valuation approaches say about that score and then finish with a way to think about value that goes beyond the usual ratios and models.

Allegiant Travel delivered 33.7% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Airlines industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Allegiant Travel Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For Allegiant Travel, the model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of about $204.9 million, and analyst inputs plus extrapolations point to free cash flow moving to $336.8 million by 2028. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extends the projections out to 2035 using the earlier years as a guide, with the individual annual free cash flows ranging from under $300 million to under $3.7b before discounting.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted back, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $1,594.26 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$111.30, this DCF outcome implies the stock is 93.0% undervalued on this framework.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Allegiant Travel is undervalued by 93.0%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Allegiant Travel.

Approach 2: Allegiant Travel Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings can be volatile, the P/S ratio is a useful cross check because it looks at what investors are paying for each dollar of revenue rather than profit, which can swing with fuel costs, one off items or accounting adjustments.

Growth expectations and risk still matter here, because a higher growth, lower risk airline might reasonably trade at a higher “normal” P/S multiple than a slower growing, higher risk peer, even if current sales are similar.

Allegiant Travel is trading on a P/S of 0.76x, compared with the Airlines industry average of 0.67x and a peer average of 0.52x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Allegiant Travel is 0.76x, which is its proprietary estimate of what the P/S could be given factors like earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk profile.

The Fair Ratio aims to be more tailored than simple peer or industry comparisons because it adjusts for company specific characteristics rather than assuming all airlines should trade at the same multiple.

With Allegiant Travel’s actual P/S of 0.76x almost identical to the Fair Ratio of 0.76x, this framework points to the shares being priced about in line with those fundamentals.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT
